entries.description: Confirmed prerequisites at session start: Set 047 closed 2026-05-26 and Set 048 closed 2026-05-27 (both spec-required predecessors). Operator answers via AskUserQuestion: (1) NTE budget $10 for Set 049 (matches Set 048 pattern), (2) Audit-S1 feature roll-call surfaced inline rather than batched at end (operator-accountability mechanism added 2026-05-27 to the Set 049 stub memory).

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped from not-started to in-progress; orchestrator block populated. Note: a Codex hook fired during the session and rewrote the orchestrator block to engine=codex/model=gpt-5.4/effort=medium mid-work — exactly the unreliable-data pattern that triggered Set 049 to begin with. Left as-is per operator-locked premise P3 (Set 049 rip-out will fix this).

Pre-audit survey grounded the audit in 4 areas: (1) read-sites for the 3 dropped fields — 18 Python files + ~20 TS files reference chatSessionId/checkedOutAt/lastActivityAt; all readers outside the coordination path are themselves retiring. (2) joiner conflict-kinds — surveyed conflicts.py to surface the discovered collisions D1/D2/D3. (3) hook + CLI invocation path — claude-session-start-invoker.js currently passes --chat-session-id from SessionStart payload + hard-codes engine/provider + recovers model/effort from prior block. (4) coordination enforcement gate — DABBLER_ENFORCE_CHECKOUT_COORDINATION env var disables H3 refusal by default since Set 046.

Pass A authored proposal.md at docs/proposals/2026-05-27-set-049-orchestrator-coordination-removal/ covering operator-locked premises P1-P5, open topics T1-T7, discovered collisions D1-D3, read-site survey, and 5 feature roll-call items. Pass A routed to gemini-pro (primary author framing: agreeing with the operator that rip-out is right, focused on disposing topics). Cost $0.0175. Result at pass-a.md: v4-compatible (T1), accept-and-ignore (T2), retire writer-log (T5), retire emit (T6), retire all 3 conflict detectors with writer-bypass decoupled+kept (D3), all 5 FRs Keep (1 Defer for FR2).

Pass B (devil's advocate) initially routed to gpt-5-4 which timed out (read operation timeout after 3 attempts) — matching gpt-5-4-pivot-to-gemini memory pattern. Fallback chain ran gpt-5-4-mini first, which succeeded. Cost $0.0300. Result at pass-b.md: counter-positions on T1 (prefer v5 bump), T2 (accept-with-warning), T4 (don't rely on readers-ignore-extras), T5 (keep writer-log), T6 (compat window), session arc estimate 5-7 not 3-5. Identified survey gap (non-runtime consumers: types files, JSON schemas, consumer-repo hooks).

Authored verdict.md synthesizing Pass A + Pass B. Pass A won T1/T6, Pass B won T2/T4/T5, both agreed on T3/T7/D1/D2/D3, session arc synthesis at 5 (Pass B additions folded into S3 sweep+normalize rather than a 6th session). Cumulative routed spend $0.0475 / $10 NTE (0.48%).

Operator inline decisions via 2 AskUserQuestion batches (4 + 4 max-per-call): all 5 FRs Keep (FR1/FR2/FR3/FR4/FR5), T1 v4-compatible (confirmed), T2 accept-with-warning (Pass B won), T5 keep provisionally (Pass B won). 4 of 5 FR answers followed the Recommended path (Pass A position). The accountability mechanism added scope-discipline without forcing material spec changes.

Authored docs/session-sets/049-orchestrator-coordination-removal/spec.md replacing the STUB. Sections: operator-locked premises (P1-P5), audit-locked dispositions (T1-T7 + D1-D3), feature roll-call (5 Keep), §4 survey gap to close before S2 code-deletion, §5 session arc (5 sessions with per-session theme breakdown), §6 non-goals, §7 cross-references. Added Session Set Configuration YAML block (totalSessions: 5, prerequisites, requiresUAT: true, tier: full). Pre-populated session-state.json sessions[] with 5 entries (S1 in-progress + S2-S5 not-started placeholders matching the locked arc).

Operator surfaced two Set-048-era context-menu defects during S1 close-out review: (1) 'Copy Eval ▸' submenu label inaccurate because it contains non-evaluation entries like 'Start Next Session' — renamed user-visible to 'Copy Prompt ▸' (internal copyEval identifier preserved). (2) dabblerSessionSets.copyStartCommand.parallel command existed in copyCommand.ts but had no ActionRegistry entry — added 'Start New Parallel Session' submenu entry with new buildStartNextParallelSessionPrompt helper + dabbler.copyStartNextParallelSessionPrompt registration. Test coverage: ROW_ACTIONS membership bumped 14→15, new gating tests, copyPromptCommands tests for parallel-variant. 668 TS pass + 2 pre-existing failures unrelated.

Two commits: a520b97 (audit S1 artifacts + locked spec + state file) and e32dd85 (context-menu hygiene patch). Pushed to origin/master (5024677..e32dd85). Memory entry written at project_set_049_s1_audit_locked.md; predecessor project_set_049_stubbed.md marked SUPERSEDED in MEMORY.md index. close_session ran clean.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped session 2 to in-progress; orchestrator block populated (still in pre-rip 7-field shape via the legacy writer at this point — rewritten to 4-field omit-null mid-session once the writer reshape landed).

§4 pre-flight survey closed the Pass B gap across 4 non-runtime areas: (1) sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts — ConflictKind Literal + RowPayload.accordionUpdatedAt vestigialities defer to S4 (Extension TS cleanup), not blockers. (2) HarvestService.ts — parseConflicts silently filters unknown kinds, graceful degradation after S2 narrows the joiner CLI output. NOT BLOCKING. (3) Consumer-repo hooks (platform / harvester / homehealthcare-accessdb) — grep for chat-session-id and chatSessionId returns zero matches in all three; T2 accept-with-warning ships as safety blanket. (4) dump_session_state_schema.py — confirmed hit (build_example_state emits the 3 retiring fields; committed reference would drift). Added inline to S2 scope as a trivial addition. Findings written to docs/session-sets/049-orchestrator-coordination-removal/s2-survey-findings.md.

register_session_start signature changes: orchestrator_chat_session_id parameter dropped; orchestrator_model and orchestrator_effort made Optional[str] = None (no 'unknown' default). Orchestrator-block construction rewritten as 4-field omit-null dict per P2: {engine} plus conditional provider/model/effort keys when caller supplied a value. The prior_orch_for_holder_check derivation, v3+v4 same-holder re-attach branches, chat_session_id_matches predicate, and checked_out_at/lastActivityAt=now emission lines all dropped. Comment block citing Set 033 H3 + Set 036 Q5 replaced with Set 049 P1-P3 spec citation. Live writer smoke test confirms the 4-field shape (subset call with engine only renders {engine: 'claude'}).

ai_router/start_session.py rewritten end-to-end. Removed: constants EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT/EXIT_READ_ONLY/CHAT_SESSION_ID_ENV_VAR/ENFORCE_COORDINATION_ENV_VAR; helpers _coordination_enforced/_identity_label/_prompt_takeover_choice/_is_interactive_tty/_log_force_override/_resolve_chat_session_id; the ~100-line H3+H4 refusal branch (prior-block check, engine_provider_match+chat_session_id_matches derivation, take-over/read-only TTY prompt, exit paths, force-override audit log on handoff); --force flag (only consumer was H3 force-override). Added: _warn_chat_session_id_ignored (T2 accept-with-warning — stderr deprecation line per supply); _log_session_start (T5 — generic 'start_session ran' record at ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log). Retained: --chat-session-id flag (value ignored by writer); --model and --effort flags as optional with no unknown default (T3).

Deleted ai_router/new_chat_id.py whole-file (no preservation flag per spec §5 S2 directive). Companion ai_router/tests/test_new_chat_id.py also deleted. T2 accept-with-warning at start_session is the sole BC surface; consumer-repo invokers that historically sourced $CHAT_SESSION_ID from new_chat_id --export are not affected because grep confirmed zero consumer-repo callers.

ai_router/close_session.py::_peek_orchestrator_identity drops chatSessionId from its return dict per P3. Docstring + the calling-site comment in _run_close updated: per-session orchestrator block is historical record under v4, not a check-out flag, so the pre-flip read for audit symmetry stays. The closeout_succeeded event payload reduces from {chatSessionId, engine, provider, model} to {engine, provider, model}. Spec §5 S2 'check-in branch removed' was effectively a documentation-only directive — under v4 the writer never nulled the block on close (that was a v3-era behavior), so no code branch needed removal beyond the docstring tightening.

ai_router/joiner/conflicts.py rewritten. Deleted: detect_engine_mismatch (D2), detect_bare_or_stale_touch (D1 bare-touch + D2 stale-checkout-touch), _touches_workspace helper, DEFAULT_ENGINE_MISMATCH_WINDOW + DEFAULT_STALENESS_THRESHOLD constants. ConflictKind Literal narrowed from 4-kind union to Literal['writer-bypass']. Module docstring + detect_writer_bypass docstring reframed as engine-independent writer-discipline check (D3 decoupled from coordination framing per spec disposition). scan_conflicts signature loses claude_root/copilot_root/engine_mismatch_window/staleness_threshold params (joiner CLI was already on the writer-bypass-only subset, no CLI edit needed).

Surveyed for holder_change / checkout_conflict event-type emit sites across the whole repo. The literal event-type strings appear ONLY in test names (about EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT, not the event type), in the audit proposal, and in the spec — they were never emitted by any code path. T6 is therefore documentation-only; no code edits needed. The closeout_succeeded event type continues to be emitted with the engine/provider/model audit payload (chatSessionId dropped at step 6).

ai_router/scripts/dump_session_state_schema.build_example_state() reshaped to emit the 4-field omit-null orchestrator block on the example sessions (chatSessionId/checkedOutAt/lastActivityAt removed). docs/session-state-schema-example.json regenerated via PYTHONPATH=ai_router;ai_router/scripts python invocation matching the conftest shim. Byte-deterministic drift check passes (format_example(state) == reference contents).

Retired (whole-file delete): test_chatsessionid_writer.py, test_checkout_writer.py, test_start_session_takeover_prompt.py, test_new_chat_id.py. Updated: test_joiner_conflicts.py rewritten to cover only writer-bypass + scan_conflicts walks emitting writer-bypass kinds (removed all engine-mismatch / bare-touch / stale-checkout-touch test classes + synthetic_workspace fixture). test_session_state_v4_writers.py::test_in_progress_session_carries_per_session_metadata loses the orchestrator_chat_session_id kwarg + 3-field assertions; added new test_orchestrator_block_applies_omit_null regression test (engine-only subset call → {engine: 'claude'}). test_no_router_backcompat.py: --force-asserting start_session tests replaced with --chat-session-id accept-with-warning tests. Full ai_router test suite: 952 pass + 1 skip + 0 regressions in 224 seconds.

Committed S2 code deliverable (1e5f53c). Net diff: -2916 lines (3266 deletions, 350 insertions) across 14 files. 4 whole-file deletes + 5 modified Python files + 4 test edits + 1 schema example. Survey-findings document (s2-survey-findings.md) committed alongside the close-out artifacts in a separate commit per the S1 pattern.
